name: Triple-Gelatin Fruit Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 cup halved orange sections (from 2 navel oranges)
2 cups bite-size pieces peeled fresh pears mixed with 2 tablespoons lemon juice
recipeInstructions:
Rinse a 10- to 12-cup fluted tube pan or ring mold with cold water.
Orange Layer:
1. Place orange gelatin in a medium metal bowl.
2. Add boiling water and stir until completely dissolved.
3. Stir in ice water and orange peel.
4. Half-fill a large bowl with ice cubes and cold water.
5. Place the bowl with gelatin in the ice bath.
6. Stir gelatin occasionally with a rubber spatula until the mixture is thick as unbeaten egg whites.
7. Remove the bowl of gelatin, fold in orange segments, and pour into the mold, distributing oranges evenly.
8. Put the mold in the freezer until the gelatin sets (about 10 minutes).
Cranberry Layer:
1. Using cranberry gelatin, follow steps 1-5 above, omitting orange peel and adding bananas in place of orange segments.
2. Pour the mixture evenly over the orange layer.
3. Return to the freezer until set (about 10 minutes).
Creamy Lemon Layer:
1. Place lemon gelatin in a medium metal bowl.
2. Add boiling water and stir until completely dissolved.
3. In another bowl, whisk cream cheese, sugar, and lemon peel until smooth.
4. Whisk the mixture into the lemon gelatin until well blended.
5. Cool the mixture as directed above.
6. When the gelatin has thickened, fold in pears.
7. Pour evenly over the cranberry layer and refrigerate at least 4 hours (or up to 2 days) until very firm.
